title: useForm Hook
description:
Implement a **useForm** custom hook that provides full form state management with field-level validation, dirty/touched tracking, and submission handling.

### Requirements

- `useForm<T>(config)` where config includes `initialValues: T` and `validate: (values: T) => Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>`
- Returns:
  - `values: T` — current form values
  - `errors: Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>` — validation errors per field
  - `touched: Partial<Record<keyof T, boolean>>` — which fields have been blurred
  - `dirty: boolean` — true if any value differs from initialValues
  - `isValid: boolean` — true when no validation errors exist
  - `getFieldProps(name: keyof T)` — returns `{ value, onChange, onBlur, name }` to spread onto an input
  - `handleSubmit(onSubmit: (values: T) => void)` — returns a form onSubmit handler that validates, prevents default, and calls onSubmit if valid
  - `resetForm()` — resets to initialValues, clears touched & errors
  - `setFieldValue(name: keyof T, value: any)` — programmatically set a field's value
- Validation runs on every value change and on blur
- `errors` only shows for fields that have been **touched**

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Name input | `field-name` |
| Email input | `field-email` |
| Name error | `error-name` |
| Email error | `error-email` |
| Submit button | `submit` |
| Reset button | `reset` |
| Dirty indicator | `dirty` |
| Valid indicator | `valid` |
constraints:
- Implement in useForm.ts as a standalone hook
- Use only React built-in hooks
- getFieldProps must return stable-ish references (avoid creating new objects on every render where possible)
- All form state must be internal — no external state management libraries
- handleSubmit must call preventDefault on the form event
hints: Keep values, touched, and errors as separate useState objects. Run validate(values) to derive errors on every values change.
getFieldProps returns an object with value, onChange (update values + run validation), onBlur (mark touched), and name.
For dirty, compare current values against initialValues using JSON.stringify or a shallow comparison.
handleSubmit should return a function that receives the form event — this allows you to spread it as <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)}>.
